plants.description: A pale cream to white-flowered form of the California poppy, offering a softer, moonlit effect in wildflower plantings. Shares the same drought tolerance and self-seeding habit as the species.
A deep carmine-pink cultivar of the California poppy, adding warm rosy tones to wildflower borders and gravel gardens. Equally drought tolerant and free-seeding as the classic orange species.
A classic giant sunflower reaching 8 feet or more with enormous golden-yellow flower heads up to 12 inches across. A bold statement plant that attracts pollinators and birds.

Known as 'Rosa de Jamaica' in Spanish-speaking countries, this tropical annual is grown for its fleshy, deep crimson calyces used to make herbal teas and drinks. It also bears attractive pale yellow flowers with a dark centre.
A mid-height ornamental onion with delicate blue flower spheres that bloom in early summer. Excellent for adding cool tones to borders.
A compact ornamental onion with broad grey-green leaves and flat white flower heads in spring. Very drought-tolerant and excellent for rock gardens.
A compact agastache with peachy-apricot flowers and aromatic foliage. Excellent for containers and small garden spaces while maintaining the hummingbird-attracting habit.
A warm red-coral agastache with aromatic foliage and extended summer-to-fall bloom. Heat and drought tolerant, prized for attracting hummingbirds and pollinators.
A tall perennial rudbeckia with bright golden-yellow flowers and a greenish-yellow center, blooming late into autumn. Excellent for cutting and back-of-border planting.
A Big Sky series coneflower with large, fragrant, warm-orange flowers over brown-tinted central cones. Upright and sturdy, blooming mid to late summer; drought-tolerant and loved by butterflies.
Black Adder is a dwarf agastache with deep purple-burgundy flower spikes and very aromatic foliage. It is compact and tidy, ideal for small spaces while attracting hummingbirds and butterflies.
A popular hybrid agastache producing tall spikes of lavender-blue flowers from mid-summer to autumn. Attracts butterflies and hummingbirds, with aromatic foliage.
A striking cultivar with silvery-frosted foliage and blue forget-me-not-like flowers in spring. The metallic leaf markings brighten shaded areas year-round.
A variegated cultivar with silvery-white heart-shaped leaves and blue spring flowers. Provides excellent foliage interest in shade gardens.

A spreading, drought-tolerant California native bearing masses of tubular scarlet-orange flowers from midsummer into autumn, beloved by hummingbirds. Formerly known as Zauschneria californica; thrives in hot, dry, sunny spots.

The classic tawny orange daylily, widely naturalised across roadsides and gardens, bearing bold orange trumpet flowers in early summer. Extremely vigorous and drought-tolerant, it spreads readily and is ideal for naturalising on banks or difficult spots.

plants.botanicalName: Veronica austriaca ssp. teucrium 'Crater Lake Blue'

plants.description: A fragrant perennial with soft peach-orange flower spikes that bloom mid-summer through fall. Attracts hummingbirds and butterflies and tolerates dry conditions once established.
A deep crimson hollyhock with wine-dark flowers that stands out as a landscape feature. This biennial or short-lived perennial brings bold color and vertical accent to borders.
A fully double-flowered hollyhock with ruffled petals in soft pink tones, creating a peony-like bloom. The layered blooms make this cultivar particularly ornamental.
A warm-toned hollyhock variety with peachy-apricot blooms that add soft color to the garden. This biennial or short-lived perennial is easy to grow and beloved in traditional cottage gardens.
A tall native perennial with purple daisy-like flowers blooming in late summer and fall. Attracts butterflies and bees, and thrives in full sun with moderate moisture.
A soft pink cultivar of New England Aster with a more compact habit than the species. Blooms prolifically in late summer and fall, attracting butterflies and bees.
A soft pink cultivar of New England Aster with delicate daisy-like flowers blooming in late summer and fall. Tall and airy, perfect for the back of borders.
A vibrant cultivar with warm orange-gold petals and a prominent central cone. Blooms prolifically and attracts pollinators throughout the growing season.
A popular daylily producing warm salmon-pink flowers with a lighter midrib over a long summer season. Vigorous and easy to grow, it is a staple of the cottage-style border.
A striking daylily with deep crimson-red flowers marked with a yellow midrib, blooming in early to midsummer. A long-established favourite for adding bold colour to the herbaceous border.
Sunrise is an agastache cultivar with warm apricot-orange flower spikes and bright green foliage. It blooms reliably from early summer through fall and is heat and drought tolerant.
A compact agastache with vibrant orange-red flower spikes that bloom from early summer through fall. Highly fragrant and a reliable pollinator magnet in sunny gardens.

A repeat-blooming form of the Damask rose, one of the oldest roses known to rebloom, producing fragrant deep-pink flowers in early summer and again in autumn. Also known as the 'Four Seasons Rose' or 'Rose of Castile'.

Bathsheba is a David Austin climbing rose with large, full rosettes in soft apricot-yellow with a paler reverse and a strong myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering and disease-resistant, good for walls, obelisks and mixed borders.
A hybrid tea rose producing fragrant, lavender-mauve blooms on long stems ideal for cutting. Moderately vigorous with good disease resistance and continuous flowering from late spring through fall.

A Meilland floribunda (sold as Bolero) with very full, creamy-white blooms flushed pale pink at the centre and a fresh, fruity fragrance. Repeat-flowering and disease-resistant, well suited to borders and cottage gardens.
A Delbard shrub rose bearing large, full, soft-pink blooms with a pleasant fragrance. Repeat-flowering from late spring through autumn with good disease resistance.
A classic rambling rose introduced in 1901, producing large clusters of small, double, rose-pink blooms once in midsummer. It is vigorous and ideal for covering pergolas, fences, and arches.
A popular climbing rose bearing clusters of deep crimson-red, double blooms with a light fragrance throughout summer and into autumn. It is a strong grower well suited to walls, fences, and pillars.
A hybrid tea rose producing large, high-centred blooms in warm peach-pink tones with a sweet fragrance. It is a vigorous, repeat-flowering variety with excellent disease resistance.

A classic moss rose with heavily mossed buds that open to soft pink, quartered blooms with a rich fragrance. It is a once-flowering old garden rose that makes a graceful, arching shrub in summer borders.

Ebb Tide is a compact shrub rose producing fragrant, deep purple-red blooms from spring through fall. It offers excellent disease resistance and is ideal for small gardens, containers, and landscape borders.

A modern shrub rose (Heirloom Roses, 1996) with fully double, old-fashioned blooms in an intense coppery-orange to apricot and a warm fragrance. Bronze-green foliage, upright habit, repeat-flowering and disease-resistant.
Madame Anisette is a modern shrub rose (Tantau) with full, apricot-cream to soft-blush blooms and a strong anise, liquorice and myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.

A compact Japanese shrub rose bearing clusters of fragrant, pink flowers from late spring through fall. Hardy and disease-resistant, it thrives in full sun with moderate water and is valued for its graceful form and long bloom season.
A David Austin English rose with full, ivory to buttermilk-white rosettes that fade almost to white, carrying a strong Old Rose and myrrh fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.
A Meilland hybrid tea, named for Princess Charlene of Monaco, with large, very full blooms in soft apricot-pink and a strong fruity fragrance. Repeat-flowering with good disease resistance.
A centifolia rose with quartered, pale pink flowers and a strong fragrance, blooming once in early summer. An heirloom variety prized for its romantic, full blooms.
A hybrid musk rose with soft pink, fragrant flowers that bloom repeatedly throughout the season. Vigorous and disease-resistant with a bushy, spreading habit.
A hybrid tea rose with vibrant orange-coral blooms and exceptional fragrance, flowering repeatedly from late spring to autumn. Compact and upright with excellent disease resistance.
A hybrid perpetual rose with large, pure white, double flowers and a strong fragrance, blooming repeatedly throughout the season. Vigorous and hardy with excellent garden presence.

A well-known Damask rose cultivar producing an exceptionally long flush of large, loosely double, warm-pink blooms with a rich, classic rose fragrance. One of the most reliable and long-flowering of the old Damask roses.

A celebrated white Damask rose bearing perfectly quartered, pure white blooms with a distinctive green button eye and a strong, fresh fragrance. Considered one of the finest white old garden roses ever bred.

The famous 'Kazanlik' or Bulgarian rose, grown commercially in the Rose Valley of Bulgaria for the production of attar of roses. It bears loosely double, vivid pink blooms of extraordinary fragrance on vigorous, arching canes.

A classic old garden rose bearing clusters of intensely fragrant, semi-double to double soft-pink blooms on arching, thorny canes. Prized for centuries for its perfume and used commercially in the production of rose oil (attar of roses).

A miniature centifolia rose dating to the 18th century, bearing tiny, perfectly formed, pompon-style blooms in soft rose-pink with a sweet fragrance. It forms a neat, twiggy bush ideal for cottage gardens and borders.

A compact, bushy Damask/Portland rose producing densely petalled, deep fuchsia-crimson blooms with a strong, classic rose fragrance. It repeat-blooms reliably through summer and autumn, making it one of the best old roses for small gardens.
A vigorous shrub rose with clusters of creamy-white, single flowers and glossy foliage. Highly disease-resistant and reliable, flowering repeatedly throughout the season.
A tall shrub rose with brilliant scarlet-red single flowers followed by decorative orange hips. Once-flowering but spectacular in bloom and valued for its ornamental fruit.
An English shrub rose with semi-double, cupped blooms in soft pink and a strong, sweet fragrance. Vigorous grower that flowers repeatedly.
A hardy, compact shrub rose with pale pink, double flowers and a strong fragrance. Flowers continuously from spring to autumn and tolerates poor soil and cold climates.
A deciduous shrub with large, pure white flower panicles from early summer through fall. Extremely popular for its abundant, long-lasting blooms and easy care.

A compact, spreading Fremontodendron cultivar that grows wider than tall, making it excellent as a large-scale groundcover or bank stabiliser. Bears the same showy golden-orange flowers as the species but in a more manageable, low-arching form.

A vigorous hybrid Fremontodendron cultivar producing an exceptionally long display of large, deep golden-orange flowers from spring into summer. One of the most floriferous selections, ideal for dry slopes and walls in mild climates.

A fast-growing California native evergreen shrub or small tree covered in large, brilliant golden-yellow saucer-shaped flowers in spring and early summer. Extremely drought tolerant once established; dislikes summer irrigation and heavy clay soils.

One of the most popular California lilac cultivars, smothered in rich cobalt-blue flower clusters in spring against glossy dark-green evergreen foliage. Highly drought tolerant once established and excellent for wildlife.
A deciduous shrub with large, cone-shaped flower panicles that open creamy-white and fade to pink in autumn. Extremely popular for its long bloom season and reliable performance in most climates.
A semi-evergreen shrub with lime-green and white variegated foliage and small, fragrant white flowers. Commonly used for hedging and ornamental screening.

Miyako is a compact Japanese maple cultivar prized for its fine, deeply-lobed red foliage that deepens in autumn. It grows slowly into a graceful, spreading shrub suitable for woodland gardens and containers.
A tropical citrus tree with fragrant white flowers followed by small, green limes. Grown for both ornamental white blooms and edible fruit in warm climates.
